howItWorks.intro: This candle wax calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Wax Weight
rules.explanation: Wax is lighter than water, so use 86% of container volume.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much wax per container?
faq.answer: Multiply container fluid ounces by 0.86 to get wax weight in ounces.

faq.question: What is the max fragrance load?
faq.answer: Most waxes handle 6-10%. Exceeding the limit causes sweating and poor burns.

faq.question: Is the Candle Wax Weight Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What container size should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate container size value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Candle Wax Weight Calculator?
sections.body: The Candle Wax Weight Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your container size, number of candles, fragrance load, you get instant results including wax needed, fragrance oil, wicks needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting wax needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your container size and need to find the right wax needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Container Size (fl oz)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your container size in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Number of Candles
howToSteps.description: Type or select your number of candles in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Fragrance Load (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your fragrance load in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your wax needed, fragrance oil, wicks needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your container size before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both wax needed and fragrance oil — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your projects.
If you're unsure about your fragrance load, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
